special1.label: Special Board 1
special1.summary: Chest zone first, trap wall second, red-upgrade zone last. Board 1 is a staging board: load early chest payload, stage near tiles 24–30, cross from a better wall position, then cash red upgrades after the wall.

strategies.label: Special 1: Staging Loaded
strategies.summary: Best tested Board 1 route. Load early chest payload, deliberately stage near tiles 24–30, then cross and convert owned chests with post-wall red upgrades.

offsetProtection.note: Protect correction cards while loading/staging; spend them when they create a real staging/cross line.

rules.targetingModel: owned upgrade cards highest eligible rarity first, random within that bucket

walkthrough.heading: Load early chest payload
steps: Treat early chests as upgradeable bodies, not just their current rarity.
Loaded route wants enough owned chest slots before committing to the wall.
Life and movement are valuable if they help you stage or cross.

walkthrough.heading: Stage near tiles 24–30
steps: Do not only ask whether you can cross immediately.
Move into the staging band when payload is loaded.
The staging band gives the wall solver much better odds than crossing from too far back.

walkthrough.heading: Cross and cash
steps: Once staged, prioritize a real wall solve to tile 37+.
After the wall, red upgrades convert owned chests toward rainbow.
If the wall succeeds, Board 1 behaves like a very premium special board.

perStartingHand.note: Board 1 is not very hand-size sensitive after staging; staging behavior matters more than +1 card.

strategies.label: Special 1: Rainbow Wall
strategies.summary: Older immediate-wall route. Kept for comparison, but the staging test shows it under-crosses Board 1 badly.

walkthrough.heading: Chest zone
steps: Treat early chests as upgradeable slots, not just their current rarity.
Green and blue chests can be correct if the wall and red upgrades look reachable.
Take life and movement tiles that make the wall solve more realistic.

walkthrough.heading: Trap wall
steps: Before crossing, solve the current hand to tile 37+.
Cross only when trap hits plus remaining life make sense.
Do not enter the wall just because it is reachable; owned upgrade payoff must justify it.

walkthrough.heading: Red upgrade zone
steps: After the wall, red upgrade tiles convert owned chests toward rainbow.
Keep recommendations simple: target high upgrade gain, reachable red upgrades, and avoid pointless empty pathing.

metrics.targetingModel: highest eligible rarity first, random within bucket
metrics.note: No-staging route tested around 30 EV versus about 91 EV for staging.

strategies.label: Special 1: Safer Wall
strategies.summary: Conservative legacy wall route. Use the staging route unless you specifically want old no-staging behavior.

metrics.targetingModel: highest eligible rarity first, random within bucket

wallStagingFinding.oldFailure: Previous route only asked whether it could cross immediately and under-crossed badly.
wallStagingFinding.newFinding: Wall staging increased realistic 4–6 EV from about 30 to about 91 in the sim.

special2.label: Special Board 2
special2.summary: Board 2 is wall-access-gated and heavily affected by starting hand size. With realistic 4–6 starting cards, Bank & Cross is the default. Unlike Board 1, staging does not freely improve EV because delay can weaken the upgrade/access balance. At 7+ cards or with strong confusion-card setup, the board becomes much more premium and Aggro Cross can compete.

strategies.label: Special 2: Bank & Cross
strategies.summary: Default Board 2 route for realistic 4–6 starting-card runs. Bank red upgrades, preserve enough access to cross, then cash the late chest zone. The wall is the run.

handManagement.principle: Movement cards are access; red upgrade cards are payoff. Extra movement cards can clog the hand.
handManagement.targetMovementBeforeWall: keep only enough movement to solve access or wall crossing
handManagement.redUpgradePriority: red upgrade cards are preferred payoff slots once access is adequate
handManagement.testedHeavyHandPressure: underperformed in 1,000-run directional test; current default keeps this as guidance, not a hard penalty

metrics.targetingModel: highest eligible rarity first, random within bucket

perStartingHand.note: Board 2 is meaningfully hand-size sensitive; 6 cards is much healthier than 4–5, while 7+ is a rare/high-setup case.

walkthrough.heading: Upgrade zone
steps: Bank red upgrade cards first.
Do not spend all movement just to grab one more upgrade.
Life and movement tiles matter because the chest zone is behind the wall.

walkthrough.heading: Wall
steps: Cross after enough upgrades are banked and life is high enough.
Board 2 may use survival crossing instead of requiring a perfect deterministic wall solve.
Do not cross early if too many red upgrades are still easy to collect.

walkthrough.heading: Chest zone
steps: Use banked red upgrades on future chest tiles as they enter range.
Every late chest is a possible conversion target.

strategies.label: Special 2: Chest Zone Fill
strategies.summary: Cross earlier and fill more chest slots. Lower value ceiling, better 7-chest rate in the test.

handManagement.principle: Movement cards are access; red upgrade cards are payoff. Extra movement cards can clog the hand.
handManagement.targetMovementBeforeWall: keep only enough movement to solve access or wall crossing
handManagement.redUpgradePriority: red upgrade cards are preferred payoff slots once access is adequate
handManagement.testedHeavyHandPressure: underperformed in 1,000-run directional test; current default keeps this as guidance, not a hard penalty

metrics.targetingModel: highest eligible rarity first, random within bucket

perStartingHand.note: Safer wall/completion style, but lower EV because it sacrifices payoff.

strategies.label: Special 2: Aggro Cross
strategies.summary: Variant for unusually large starting hands or high-access/confusion setups. Not the default for normal 4–6 card starts.

handManagement.principle: Movement cards are access; red upgrade cards are payoff. Extra movement cards can clog the hand.
handManagement.targetMovementBeforeWall: keep only enough movement to solve access or wall crossing
handManagement.redUpgradePriority: red upgrade cards are preferred payoff slots once access is adequate
handManagement.testedHeavyHandPressure: underperformed in 1,000-run directional test; current default keeps this as guidance, not a hard penalty

metrics.targetingModel: highest eligible rarity first, random within bucket
metrics.highHandNote: At 8 starting cards, Aggro edged Bank in the test, but 8-card starts are not baseline realistic.

perStartingHand.note: Close to Bank at 4–6, but Bank wins the realistic baseline; Aggro is mainly for high-hand/high-access starts.

startingHandImpact.baselineNote: User reports 4–6 starting cards are roughly 98% of realistic starts.
startingHandImpact.lowBand: 4–5 cards: lower floor; wall failure drags average EV hard.
startingHandImpact.highBand: 7+ cards: rare outside strong RNG/confusion setup; EV rises sharply and Aggro Cross can compete.
startingHandImpact.variantNote: Use Special2_Aggro_Cross for unusually large starting hands/high-access setups.

stagingFinding.finding: Board 2 staging increased wall success but did not improve EV in the 4–6 card test.
stagingFinding.reason: Staging can consume life/time/access before the chest zone, weakening payoff.

special3.label: Special Board 3
special3.summary: Special Board 3 is a slot-preservation board, not a wall board. Chest rarity bands climb from green to rainbow across the board, so the main mistake is filling the 7 chest slots too early with low bands. Default route aggressively preserves slots for red/rainbow bands; safer variants fill more consistently with orange+.

boardShape.chestsPerRow: 2 random chests per row before movement overwrite

finding.coreRule: Preserve slots for the late bands.
finding.mistake: Greedy completion fills 7 slots too early with green/blue/purple and misses red/rainbow value.

strategies.label: Special 3: Rainbow/Red High Ceiling
strategies.summary: Highest tested EV route. Skip low bands aggressively, use movement to reach red/rainbow bands, and accept lower 7-chest completion for higher average value.

rules.minCollectBand: red+ preferred, orange allowed as filler
rules.upgradeCardUse: future-first, highest eligible rarity first, random within bucket

walkthrough.heading: Early bands
steps: Do not fill 7 slots in green/blue/purple.
Use movement and pathing to push toward late bands.
Only take low bands when the route would otherwise brick or needs filler.

walkthrough.heading: Middle bands
steps: Orange is acceptable filler, but do not let it crowd out red/rainbow.
Movement pickups before red/rainbow are premium because they preserve access.

walkthrough.heading: Late bands
steps: Prioritize red and rainbow chests.
If you reach the rainbow band with open slots, this board spikes hard.
Upgrade cards should improve future high-band chests or owned high-value chests.

perStartingHand.note: Board 3 is highly hand-size sensitive; extra cards greatly improve access to late red/rainbow bands.

strategies.label: Special 3: Red+ Dynamic
strategies.summary: Balanced route that still preserves slots for red/rainbow but fills more reliably than the highest-ceiling route.

rules.coreRule: Prefer red+, allow orange/purple filler dynamically.
rules.upgradeCardUse: future-first, highest eligible rarity first, random within bucket

perStartingHand.note: Balanced EV route also scales strongly with starting hand size.

strategies.label: Special 3: Red+ Softer Fill
strategies.summary: Safer fill route. Gives up some ceiling to complete 7 chests more often while still avoiding early low-band spam.

rules.coreRule: Preserve slots, but allow filler sooner.
rules.upgradeCardUse: future-first, highest eligible rarity first, random within bucket

perStartingHand.note: Softer Fill keeps completion high while still scaling with hand size.

strategies.label: Special 3: Completion Safe
strategies.summary: Low-risk completion route. Fills 7 chests very often, but lower EV because it accepts too many mid/low slots.

rules.coreRule: Complete 7 chests reliably, but accept lower ceiling.

perStartingHand.note: Completion Safe scales less explosively because it takes more filler.
